content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

March 22, 1988

Ms. Belle Tauber
2333 Ocean Parkway
Brooklyn, New York  11223

RE:  Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-88-082

Dear Ms. Tauber:

You have requested advice under the campaign disclosure
provisions of the Political Reform Act.  1/

FACTS

Recently you lent $10,000 to the Committee to Elect
Jeffrey Tauber.  You were informed by Mr. Tauber that
individuals who make contributions (including loans)
aggregating $10,000 or more in a calendar year are required to
file campaign disclosure statements.  Mr. Tauber suggested you
contact the Fair Political Practices Commission for additional
information.

ANSWER

"Committee" means any person or combination of persons
who directly or indirectly does any of the following: (a)
receives contributions totaling one thousand dollars ($1,000)
or more in a calendar year; (b) makes independent expenditures
totaling one thousand dollars ($1,000) or more in a calendar
year; or (c) makes contributions totaling ten thousand dollars
($10,000) or more in a calendar year to or at the behest of
candidates or committees....."  (Section 82013.)

The $10,000 loan you made to the Committee to Elect
Jeffrey Tauber is considered a contribution.  (Section 82015
and 82044.)  Therefore, it qualifies you as a major donor
committee pursuant to Section 82013(c).

I have enclosed for your use a Form 461, Independent
Expenditure and Major Donor Committee Campaign Statement and a
1988 "Information Manual on Campaign Disclosure Provisions of

_____________
1/Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise
indicated.  Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code
of Regulations Section 18000, et seq.  All references to
regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code
of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Belle Tauber
Page 2

the Political Reform Act for Major Donor Committees and
Independent Expenditure Committees."  Please see page 4 for
guidelines on when campaign statements must be filed by major
donor committees and page 11 to determine where you must file
the Form 461.

     If you have additional questions concerning your filing
requirements, please contact me at 916/322-5662.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Diane M. Griffiths
                                        Legal Counsel

                                        [signature]

                                        By:  Mary Ann Kvasager
                                             Political Reform Consultant

Enclosures
DMG:MAK

F P P

FEB 22 3 2YtH'88                                    February 9, 1988
                                                    2333 Ocean Parkway
                                                    Brooklyn, New York 11223

Fair Political Practices Commission
Eighth Floor
428 J Street
Sacramento, CA 95814


Dear Commissioners:

I have recently made a loan of $10,000 to "The Committee to
Elect Jeffrey Tauber". Their campaign committee number is
880048.

I am enclosing the letter which I was sent instructing me
to request information from your office. Please send me
the necessary materials as soon as possible.

Thank you very much.

Belle Tauber
Belle Tauber

Tauber & Frederick
Attorneys at Law
250 GRAND AVENUE • 3rd FLOOR • OAKLAND, CALIFORNIA 94610
(415) 854-4658

Jeffrey S. Tauber                                          In Association With
Joyce Frederick Larsen                                     John M. Harrigan
                                                          Lawrence L. Szabo
                                                          Gus F. Diaz
5 February 1988                                           H. Stanley Dewey
                                                          Rodney S. Low

Mrs. Belle Tauber
2333 Ocean Parkway
Brooklyn, New York 11223

Dear Mrs. Tauber:

Thank you for your recent loan of $10,000 to my election
campaign.

In California, individuals who make contributions (including
loans) totalling $10,000 or more in a calendar year are
required to file campaign statements with the Secretary of
State. Since your contribution totals $10,000, you are
required by the Political Reform Act of 1974 to file a
campaign statement. For more information, please contact:
    The Fair Political Practices Commission
    8th Floor
    428 J street, Sacramento, CA 95814
    Their telephone is (916) 322-5662
    Our campaign committee number is 880048.

I've also enclosed a copy ofthe section from the campaign
disclosure manual for your reference.

Please call if I can help you in any way.

Most sincerely,

Jeff Tauber

Tauber & Frederick
Attorneys at Law

250 GRAND AVENUE • 3rd FLOOR • OAKLAND, CALIFORNIA 94610
(415) 854-4656


Jeffrey S. Tauber                                          In Association With
Joyce Frederick Larsen                                     John M. Harrigan
                                                           Lawrence L. Szabo
                                                           Gus F. Diaz
                                                           H. Stanley Dewey
                                                           Rodney S. Low

5 February 1988

Mrs. Belle Tauber
2333 Ocean Parkway
Brooklyn, New York 11223

Dear Mrs. Tauber:

Thank you for your recent loan of $10,000 to my election
campaign.

In California, individuals who make contributions (including
loans) totaling $10,000 or more in a calendar year are
required to file campaign statements with the Secretary of
State. Since your contribution totals $10,000, you are
required by the Political Reform Act of 1974 to file a
campaign statement. For more information, please contact:
The Fair Political Practices Commission
8th Floor
428 J Street, Sacramento, CA 95814
Their telephone is (916) 322-5662
Our campaign committee number is 880048.

I've also enclosed a copy of the section from the campaign
disclosure manual for your reference.

Please call if I can help you in any way.

Most sincerely,

Jeff Tauber

BELLE TAUBER
2333 OCEAN PARKWAY
BROOKLYN, NY. 11223

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
8th FLOOR
428 J STPEET
SACRAMENTO, CA 95814

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
February 25, 1988


Belle Tauber
2333 Ocean Parkway
Brooklyn, NY 11223

Re: 88-082

Dear Ms. Tauber:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act was received on February 22, 1988 by the Fair Political
Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or more information is needed, you should expect a response
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written
advice. If more information is needed, the person assigned to
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to
advise you as to the information needed. If your request is
for informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we
can. (See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Adm. Code Sec.
18329).)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

Jeanne Pritchard
Chief
Technical Assistance and Analysis
Division

sections.question_synthetic: Does Ms. Belle Tauber qualify as a major donor committee subject to campaign disclosure filing requirements after making a $10,000 loan to the Committee to Elect Jeffrey Tauber?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Ms. Tauber's $10,000 loan is a contribution that qualifies her as a major donor committee under Government Code Section 82013(c), requiring her to file Form 461 campaign disclosure statements with the specified filing deadlines and locations.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: Document contains multiple related pieces: incoming request letter from Belle Tauber (Feb 9), preliminary letter from Jeffrey Tauber's law office (Feb 5), FPPC receipt acknowledgment (Feb 25), and formal FPPC advice letter (March 22). Extracted main advice letter as primary response. No explicit QUESTION or CONCLUSION headers; ANSWER section serves as substantive conclusion.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Does Ms. Belle Tauber qualify as a major donor committee subject to campaign disclosure filing requirements after making a $10,000 loan to the Committee to Elect Jeffrey Tauber?

CONCLUSION: The $10,000 loan you made to the Committee to Elect Jeffrey Tauber is considered a contribution. (Section 82015 and 82044.) Therefore, it qualifies you as a major donor committee pursuant to section 82013(c).

embedding.summary: This is an FPPC advice letter responding to Belle Tauber's inquiry about whether her $10,000 loan to a candidate committee requires her to file campaign disclosure statements. The FPPC advises that the loan constitutes a contribution making her a major donor committee under the Political Reform Act.
